title: Menemen (Turkish Scrambled Eggs with Peppers)

subs.name: silken tofu, crumbled — cook identically for a vegan version
subs.notes: texture differs but absorbs the sauce beautifully

subs.name: 4 tbsp tomato paste thinned with 3 tbsp water
zucchini or brinjal, finely diced
subs.notes: adds a different but pleasant sweetness

ingredients.name: onion, finely diced
visual_checks: traditionalists omit onion; add it for extra body
ingredients.notes: traditionalists omit onion; add it for extra body

steps.text: Heat oil or butter in a small skillet or kadai on medium flame. If using onion, add it first and cook for 3–4 minutes until soft. Add the capsicum and green chilli, cooking for another 3 minutes until the capsicum softens.
Add the diced tomatoes, a pinch of salt and chilli powder. Cook on medium for 5–6 minutes, stirring, until the tomatoes break down into a chunky, jammy sauce and most of the moisture evaporates.
Reduce to the lowest flame. Crack the eggs directly over the vegetables and stir gently and continuously with a wooden spoon or spatula — do not beat the eggs before adding.
Fold the eggs slowly with the vegetable mixture for 2–3 minutes until they just set into soft, creamy curds. Remove from heat while still slightly underdone — residual heat will finish the cooking.
Season with salt, scatter herbs on top and serve immediately in the pan with crusty bread or pide for scooping.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: capsicum → zucchini or brinjal, finely diced (adds a different but pleasant sweetness); tomato → 4 tbsp tomato paste thinned with 3 tbsp water (quick weekday shortcut); egg → silken tofu, crumbled — cook identically for a vegan version (texture differs but absorbs the sauce beautifully)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This is essentially a spiced egg bhurji cooked in a different sequence — cook in your smallest kadai on a gas hob. Use desi ghee instead of butter for extra richness. Serve with sliced bread, pav or paratha.
